commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And the priests and the Levites purified themselves, and purified the people, and the gates, and the wall. Were purified first, that they might purify the rest. (Haydock) Priests were obliged to abstain from wine, and from their wives, while they were on duty. See 2 Paralipomenon xxix. Levites were to wash their garments, Leviticus viii. All lepers, required a certain purification, ibid. and Numbers xix. Care was taken that no dead body was found on the walls. These were probably sprinkled with water, like the tabernacle, Leviticus viii.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
